planets.name: Sun
planets.description: The Sun is the enormous star at the centre of our solar system. Its powerful gravity holds all the planets in orbit, and the energy it releases provides the light and heat that make life on Earth possible.

facts.title: ☀ The Solar System's Powerhouse
facts.text: The Sun produces energy through nuclear fusion deep inside its core. Every second it converts millions of tons of hydrogen into helium, releasing enormous amounts of energy.

facts.title: 💡 Light Travels Fast
facts.text: Even though sunlight moves at the speed of light, it still takes about eight minutes to reach Earth because the Sun is 150 million kilometres away.

facts.title: ⚖ Almost Everything
facts.text: The Sun contains about 99.8% of all the mass in our solar system. Every planet, moon, asteroid and comet together make up only a tiny fraction compared to the Sun.

planets.name: Mercury
planets.description: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and the closest to the Sun. Because it has almost no atmosphere, temperatures swing between extremely hot days and freezing nights.

facts.title: 🚀 The Fastest Planet
facts.text: Mercury travels around the Sun faster than any other planet. A full orbit takes just 88 Earth days, meaning Mercury experiences several years in the time Earth completes one.

facts.title: 🌡 Extreme Temperature Swings
facts.text: Without an atmosphere to trap heat, Mercury's temperature changes dramatically. During the day it can reach 430°C, but at night it drops to −180°C.

facts.title: 🧊 Ice Near the Sun
facts.text: Even though Mercury is very close to the Sun, scientists have discovered water ice hidden inside deep craters near the poles where sunlight never reaches.

planets.name: Venus
planets.description: Venus is similar in size to Earth but has an extremely thick atmosphere made mostly of carbon dioxide. This atmosphere traps heat and makes Venus the hottest planet in the solar system.

facts.title: 🔥 The Hottest Planet
facts.text: Even though Mercury is closer to the Sun, Venus is hotter because its thick atmosphere traps heat like a giant greenhouse.

facts.title: 🔄 A Backwards Planet
facts.text: Venus rotates in the opposite direction of most planets. On Venus the Sun would appear to rise in the west and set in the east.

facts.title: ☁ Clouds of Acid
facts.text: The clouds of Venus are made mostly of sulfuric acid. These clouds reflect sunlight strongly, which is why Venus appears very bright in the sky.

planets.name: Earth
planets.description: Earth is the only known planet that supports life. Its atmosphere, liquid water oceans and magnetic field create the right conditions for living organisms to survive.

facts.title: 🌊 A Water World
facts.text: About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water. These oceans help regulate the planet's temperature and support countless forms of life.

facts.title: 🧲 Protective Magnetic Shield
facts.text: Earth has a strong magnetic field generated by its molten core. This magnetic shield protects the planet from harmful solar radiation.

facts.title: 🌙 Our Companion Moon
facts.text: Earth's Moon stabilizes the tilt of our planet and causes ocean tides. It also played an important role in the history of exploration when humans first landed on it in 1969.

planets.name: Mars
planets.description: Mars is often called the Red Planet because its surface is rich in iron oxide, the same material that gives rust its reddish colour.

facts.title: 🔴 The Red Planet
facts.text: Mars gets its reddish appearance from iron oxide dust that covers much of its surface. This rust-coloured dust gives the planet its famous nickname.

facts.title: 🌋 Giant Volcano
facts.text: Mars is home to Olympus Mons, the largest volcano in the entire solar system. It stands about three times taller than Mount Everest.

facts.title: 🚀 Searching for Life
facts.text: Robotic missions from Earth are currently exploring Mars to search for signs of ancient water and possible past microbial life.

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.description: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system and is made mostly of hydrogen and helium gas. Its immense gravity influences many objects throughout the solar system.

facts.title: 🌪 The Great Red Spot
facts.text: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a gigantic storm that has been raging for more than 300 years. The storm is so large that Earth could fit inside it.

facts.title: 🌙 A Moon Family
facts.text: Jupiter has more than 95 known moons. Some of the largest moons, like Europa and Ganymede, may even contain hidden oceans beneath their icy surfaces.

facts.title: 📏 Truly Enormous
facts.text: Jupiter is so large that more than 1,300 Earth-sized planets could fit inside it if it were hollow.

planets.name: Saturn
planets.description: Saturn is famous for its stunning ring system made of billions of pieces of ice and rock that orbit the planet like a giant cosmic disc.

facts.title: 💍 Spectacular Rings
facts.text: Saturn's rings are made of countless chunks of ice and rock ranging in size from tiny grains to pieces as large as mountains.

facts.title: 🪶 Extremely Light
facts.text: Saturn is the least dense planet in the solar system. If you could place it in a gigantic ocean, it would actually float.

facts.title: 🌙 Many Moons
facts.text: Saturn has more than 140 moons. One of them, Titan, has lakes and rivers made of liquid methane.

planets.name: Uranus
planets.description: Uranus is an icy giant planet known for its unusual sideways rotation. Instead of spinning upright like most planets, Uranus rotates almost completely on its side.

facts.title: 🔄 A Sideways Planet
facts.text: Uranus has an extreme tilt of about 98 degrees. This means the planet rotates almost completely on its side as it orbits the Sun.

facts.title: ❄ Freezing Atmosphere
facts.text: Uranus has the coldest atmosphere of any planet in the solar system, with temperatures dropping to about −224°C.

facts.title: 💍 Hidden Rings
facts.text: Although not as bright as Saturn's, Uranus also has a system of rings made from dark particles and debris.

planets.name: Neptune
planets.description: Neptune is the most distant planet from the Sun and is known for its deep blue colour and powerful storms.

facts.title: 🔭 Discovered with Mathematics
facts.text: Neptune was predicted using mathematics before it was ever seen through a telescope. Astronomers noticed Uranus was being pulled by something invisible.

facts.title: 💨 Fastest Winds
facts.text: Neptune has the strongest winds in the solar system. Some storms reach speeds of more than 2,000 kilometres per hour.

facts.title: 🧊 A Mysterious Ice Giant
facts.text: Despite its icy name, Neptune's interior may contain a hot, slushy ocean of water, methane and ammonia under extreme pressure.
